perturbation.original_text: All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Conf idential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall s urvive and continue for five (5) years after the date of the last discl osure of Confidential Informati on by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All obligations of Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information received from Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement shall survive and continue for two (2) years after the date of the last disclosure of Confidential Information by Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the duration of confidentiality obligations from five years to two years creates an in-text contradiction with subsequent sections that might implicitly or explicitly rely on the five-year term. This creates uncertainty about the actual duration of the confidentiality obligations. Specifically, any clause referencing the duration, or implying actions within a 5 year range is in conflict with this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immediately return (or, at the option of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductions of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans, enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Party further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or control of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance with their respective internal record retention policies for legal, compliance or regulatory purposes or to establish the rights of Receiving Party and its Representatives under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change reduces the confidentiality period from five years to two years. The contradicted text allows the Receiving Party to retain copies of Confidential Information subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement. The change directly impacts the 'restrictions on use and disclosure' by shortening the duration of confidentiality. Thus, the contradicted section becomes inconsistent with the new two-year limit. The original text permitted retaining confidential information under a 5 year restriction, but the changed text now has a 2 year restriction creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the limitations set forth herein, “ Confidential Information ” means any and all confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of Disclo sing Party (whether oral, written, electronic or otherwise), including but not limited to marketing and sales data, customer lists, customer requirements, price lists, produc t and service lists, market st udies, financial statements, business plans, the names and backgrounds of key pe rsonnel, research and developmen t, service methods, processes, improvements, devices, know-how, inventions, discoveries, conce pts, ideas, designs, methods and information, and all related notes, analyses, compilations, stu dies, specifications, designs, plans, enhancements, data of Disclosing Party and any notes or other m aterials prepared by Receiving Party or any of its partners, directors, officers, employees, agents, co ntractors, affiliates, attorneys and other advisors or representatives (collectively, “ Representatives ”) that contain or are generated from such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the limitations set forth herein, “ Confidential Information ” means only written documents explicitly marked as 'Confidential' by the Disclosing Party. This excludes oral information, electronic data, and any information not physically labeled as confidential.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only written documents explicitly marked as such, this creates an in-text contradiction with other clauses that discuss the use and protection of Confidential Information more broadly (e.g., referring to electronic data or oral disclosures). This change causes uncertainty as to which information is actually covered by the agreement and subject to its protections. For instance, the Receiving Party may argue that any information disclosed orally or electronically is not subject to the agreement's restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon receipt of any Confidential Information, Receiving Part y shall: (a) only disclose such Confidential Information to those of its Representatives with a need to have access to and knowledge of the Confidential Inform ation for the limited purpose expressly stated herein and not disclose to any other person ex cept with the prior written approval of Disclosing Party; (b) advise any of its Representatives who receive the Confident ial Information of the existence and terms of this Agreement and of the obligations of confidentiality and non- disclosure herein; cause its Representatives to comply with thi s Agreement as if they were parties hereto; and be responsible for a ny breach of this Agreement by its Representatives; (c) use and require its Representatives to use at least the sam e degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as they use to protect the ir own proprietary information, which degree of care shall not be less than reasonable care; an d
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only explicitly marked written documents, whereas the contradicted text discusses the handling of 'any Confidential Information' without such restrictions. This creates a clear contradiction because the contradicted text implies a broader scope of confidential information, including electronic and oral information, which the changed text explicitly excludes. This inconsistency would lead to confusion and potential disputes regarding which information is protected under the agreement, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receivi ng Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immedi ately return (or, at the op tion of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible or electronic Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving P arty or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductio ns of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans , enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Part y further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or co ntrol of any Confidential Information. Provided, however, that Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information, subject to the restrictions on use and disclosure contained in this Agreement, in accordance Commercial Investigations LLC Page 3 of 4 with their respective internal record retention policies for le gal, compliance or regulatory purposes or to establish the rights of Receiving Party and its Representatives under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immediately return (or, at the option of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductions of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans, enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Party further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or control of any Confidential Information. Receiving Party and its Representatives are not obligated to return or destroy electronic Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that Receiving Party is not obligated to return or destroy electronic confidential information, it creates an in-text contradiction. This contradicts the start of the text in section 8, which states that at any time all 'tangible or electronic Confidential Information' disclosed shall be destroyed. This creates uncertainty about the actual obligations of the Receiving Party regarding electronic data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon written demand by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall, and shall cause its Representatives to, immediately return (or, at the option of Disclosing Party, destroy) all tangible Confidential Information disclosed to Receiving Party or any of its Representatives under this Agreement, along with all copies, extracts and reproductions of such information and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, specifications, designs, plans, enhancements, data, prototypes, derivative works and all other works prepared by Receiving Party or any of its Representatives that are derived from or based on any of the Confidential Information. Receiving Party further agrees to promptly provide Disclosing Party with written certification of the satisfaction of the terms of this paragraph by Receiving Party and each of its Representatives that had possession or control of any Confidential Information. Receiving Party and its Representatives are not obligated to return or destroy electronic Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that upon written demand, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy all 'tangible or electronic Confidential Information'. The changed text states that Receiving Party is not obligated to return or destroy 'electronic Confidential Information'. This creates a direct conflict with the original text, leading to uncertainty regarding the obligations of the Receiving Party concerning electronic data. The contradicted text is essentially the same as the changed text, containing the contradictory statement that Receiving Party and its Representatives are not obligated to return or destroy electronic Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is not assignable and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is assignable with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Altering the assignability clause creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause explicitly prohibits assignment, while the modified clause permits it with consent. This creates uncertainty about whether the agreement can be transferred to another party and under what conditions. This could significantly impact the rights and obligations of both parties, particularly if the Disclosing Party objects to a proposed assignment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is assignable with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party and is binding on any successors of Receiving Party. If any provis ion of this Agreement is for any reason adjudged to be void, invalid or unenforceable, then the remaind er of this Agreement shall continue and remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be execute d in counterpart by facsimile, PDF or electronic signature, which shall be deemed to be an original f or evidentiary purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is 'not assignable', while the changed text makes it 'assignable with prior written consent.' The contradicted text states 'This Agreement is assignable with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party'. The changed text directly conflicts with the original text, which stated it was not assignable, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it alters a fundamental condition of the agreement, as per the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: The disclosure of any Confidential Information by Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement does not constitute a representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party with respect to the accurac y or completeness of the information.
perturbation.changed_text: The disclosure of any Confidential Information by Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement constitutes a warranty by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party that the information is accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the 'no warranty' clause to a warranty of accuracy and completeness introduces an in-text contradiction. This directly conflicts with the original intent to disclaim any responsibility for the accuracy of the disclosed information. This creates uncertainty about the Disclosing Party's liability if the information turns out to be inaccurate or incomplete and the Receiving Party relies on it to their detriment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The disclosure of any Confidential Information by Disclosing Party under the terms of this Agreement constitutes a warranty by Disclosing Party to Receiving Party that the information is accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the disclosure of confidential information does *not* constitute a warranty regarding its accuracy or completeness. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the disclosure *does* constitute a warranty of accuracy and completeness to the best of the Disclosing Party's knowledge. This creates a direct conflict within the contract, as the same action (disclosure of confidential information) is simultaneously warranted and not warranted. The contradicted text and changed text are the same, highlighting the self-contradictory nature of the modification.
